03 April 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs

Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • One Crew Member Rescued After American Jet Shot Down In Iran, US Official Tells RFE/RL RFE/RL 03 Apr 2026 -- A US crew member has been rescued by American forces after a jet fighter was shot down over Iran, a US official told RFE/RL, and the search is ongoing for a second, in the first known loss of an American plane to hostile fire since the war began.
  • 8 killed, 95 wounded in U.S.-Israeli attacks on bridge in N. Iran Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- At least eight civilians were killed and 95 others wounded in U.S. and Israeli attacks on a bridge in Iran's northern Alborz province on Thursday, Xinhua reported citing Iran's official IRNA news agency.

  • Tehran Hits Gulf Energy Sites, Trump Warns US 'Hasn't Even Started' To Destroy Iran RFE/RL 03 Apr 2026 -- Iran launched attacks across the Middle East on April 3, setting parts of a major Kuwaiti oil refinery ablaze and triggering air defense responses across the Gulf, as the war with the United States and Israel neared the end of its fifth week.
  • US-Israeli aggression on Iran: What happened on 35th day of the imposed war Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Thirty-five days into the US-Israeli war on Iran, launched on February 28 with the assassination of the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, and some top-ranking commanders, the military aggression continues to target civilian, medical and academic infrastructure across the country.
  • 'Black day for US, Israeli air forces': Iran downs 2 fighter jets, 2 missiles, 3 drones in single day Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iranian armed forces announced that its air defense forces downed at least two fighter jets and five drones and missiles on Friday, describing it as a "black day" for the American and Israeli air forces.
  • Iran's Army announces downing American A-10 Warthog warplane Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Army has announced downing of an American A-10 Warthog warplane by the Islamic Republic's integrated air defense network over the country's southern waters near the Strait of Hormuz.
  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on April 3 Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iranian armed forces and resistance groups across the region continue to carry out retaliatory military operations against the United States and the Israeli regime.
  • Wave 93: Israeli military support centers targeted in joint retaliatory strike Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iranian and allied resistance forces executed Wave 93 of their retaliatory campaign against US-Israeli aggression, dealing precise blows to critical Israeli military staging grounds deep inside the occupied territories.
  • Iran can sustain Strait of Hormuz closure for years, will cut US military logistics: Official Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- The current situation in the Strait of Hormuz could remain as it is for a long time, and Iran has the capability to sustain it for years, a senior Iranian security official told Press TV.
  • Iran launches drone attacks on US military positions, interests in West Asia Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- The Iranian army has conducted new drone attacks on US military positions in Jordan and Kuwait, as well as on an aluminum factory in Bahrain involved in the production of American military equipment.
  • IRGC: US, Israel responsible for attack on Kuwait's power, desalination plant Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC) has said that Israel carried out an earlier attack on Kuwait's power and desalination plant, warning regional countries to stay vigilant.
  • IRGC shoots down intruding Israeli cruise missile Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has shot down an intruding advanced cruise missile belonging to the Zionist regime.
  • True Promise 4: Strategic US radar, naval assets destroyed in wave 92 Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iranian armed forces targeted and destroyed strategic US radar systems and naval equipment across the region and occupied territories in the 92nd wave of Operation True Promise 4 in retaliation of US-Israeli aggression on Iran.
  • Iran downs two advanced US jets, including second F-35 Press TV 3 Apr 2026 -- Iranian forces shot down two more advanced US fighter jets today, including a second F-35, despite US President Donald Trump's claim of destroying Iran's air defenses.
  • US-Israeli aggression on Iran: What happened on 34th day of the imposed war Press TV 3 Apr 2026 -- Thirty-four days into the US-Israeli war on Iran, launched on February 28 with the assassination of the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, the military aggression continues to target civilian and industrial infrastructure across the country.

  • Iraq resistance strikes US occupation bases in six operations over past 24 hours Press TV 3 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Resistance of Iraq has announced that its fighters carried out six precise operations against American occupation bases across Iraq in the past 24 hours, dealing another series of blows to the illegal US military presence in the country.

  • Trump Seeking Way Out of Conflict With Iran Amid Upcoming Congressional Elections — Report Sputnik 03 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump is seeking a way out of the conflict with Iran, fearing that a protracted military operation could undermine Republicans' position in the midterm congressional elections, the Time magazine reported, citing sources.
  • Trump Warns US 'Hasn't Even Started' To Destroy Iran, Says Regime Must Act 'Fast' RFE/RL 3 Apr 2026 -- President Donald Trump said the US military "hasn't even started" to destroy what is left of Iran after a month of devastating air attacks and he threatened to strike electrical power plants and more bridges in his latest warning to the regime in Tehran.

  • Iran hints at huge pending pressure on enemies after Yemen threatens to close Bab el-Mandeb Strait Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- As Iran's closure of Hormuz Strait to enemy vessels has already put intense pressure on adversaries, the Islamic Republic's top legislator hints at the prospect of additional momentous pressure with potential closure of Bab el-Mandeb Strait, another vital chokehold.
  • Iran to regional hosts of US bases: Expel American forces or face crushing strikes Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- The central headquarters of the Iranian armed forces has issued a stark warning to the United States and its regional hosts after US President Donald Trump's latest threats.
  • Trump's 'no-strategy' war downgraded to a hunt for pilots in Iran: Qalibaf Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says the US-Israel's illegal war on the country has "downgraded from regime change" to a hunt for their pilots after Iran's armed forces downed another F-35 warplane earlier Friday.
  • Official reveals evidence of Arab states' involvement in US-Israeli war on Iran Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei has revealed hard evidence on the involvement of some Persian Gulf Arab states in the unprovoked US-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • IAEA's silence makes it complicit in US-Israeli attacks on Iran's nuclear facilities: AEOI Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) says the International Atomic Energy Agency's continued silence on US-Israeli attacks on the country's peaceful nuclear facilities makes the agency complicit in the aggression.
  • 'A massive war crime': Iran president denounces US 'Stone Age' threat Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has censured American threats to bomb the Islamic Republic "back to the Stone Age," warning that those who keep silent in the face of criminals will pay "a heavy price."
  • Iran's military vows to continue war until US, Israel 'regret' aggression Press TV 03 Apr 2026 -- The senior spokesman for Iran's Armed Forces says the country will continue the war as long as it makes the United States and the Israeli regime "regret" their illegal act of aggression.

  • Guterres warns of 'wider war' as Middle East conflict enters second month Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- The Middle East crisis has lurched into its second month, prompting UN Secretary-General António Guterres to issue a stark warning on Thursday morning that the world is "on the edge of a wider war" with catastrophic global implications, according to the official UN website.
  • UN Security Council Delays Vote On Use Of 'Defensive' Force In Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 3 Apr 2026 -- The UN Security Council has delayed a vote on a draft resolution to allow the use of "defensive" force to protect shipping from Iranian attacks in the crucial Strait of Hormuz.
  • UN chief renews call for U.S., Israel to stop war against Iran People's Daily 3 Apr 2026 -- United Nations (UN) Secretary-General Antonio Guterres on Thursday reiterated his call for the United States and Israel to stop the war against Iran, and for Iran to stop attacking its neighbors.

  • Chinese FM spokesperson responds to Trump's video showing strike on Iran's largest bridge aimed at pressuring Tehran into deal Global Times 03 Apr 2026 -- China opposes attacks on civilian facilities and the relevant parties should immediately cease fire, return to the track of political and diplomatic resolution, and prevent a larger humanitarian disaster, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said at a regular press briefing on Friday, in response to media inquiries over a Thursday social media video released by US President Donald Trump, showing a strike against Iran's "biggest bridge."
  • Chinese FM says UN Security Council actions should not legitimize unauthorized military operations People's Daily 3 Apr 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Thursday that UN Security Council actions must not provide legal cover for unauthorized military operations, nor should they heighten tensions or escalate conflict.

  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova regarding the damage of the St Nicholas Church in Tehran in a missile strike Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026
  • US Needs to Stop Fighting Iran, Not Talk About Problem of Strait of Hormuz - Lavrov Sputnik 03 Apr 2026 -- The United States needs to stop fighting against Iran and not talk about the problem of the blocked Strait of Hormuz, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Friday.

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Past Hours SPA 03 Apr 2026

  • Over 40 countries including Korea discuss reopening Hormuz KOREA.net 03 Apr 2026 -- Foreign ministers and representatives from more than 40 countries including Korea on April 2 discussed how to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in a U.K.-led videoconference.
  • HE GCCSG and the UN SG Discuss the Brutal Iranian Attacks on GCC States and Stress the Need for Iran to Comply with UN Resolution 2817 Gulf Cooperation Council 03 Apr 2026
  • PM call with the Crown Prince of Kuwait: 3 April 2026 Gov.UK 03 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister spoke to His Highness the Crown Prince of Kuwait, Sheikh Sabah Al-Khaled Al-Hamad Al-Mubarak Al-Sabah this morning.

  • Iran Conflict Disrupts Private US Humanitarian Aid For Ukraine RFE/RL 03 Apr 2026 -- The flow of volunteer humanitarian aid from the United States to Ukraine - a critical lifeline for frontline communities - is coming under mounting strain, as disruptions linked to the Iran conflict ripple through global shipping networks.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Chairmen of Senate and House Armed Services Committees Praise President Trump's $1.5 Trillion Defense Budget Topline House Armed Services Committee 03 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Senator Roger Wicker, (R-MS), Chairman of the Senate Armed Services Committee, and U.S. Representative Mike Rogers, (R-AL), Chairman of the House Armed Services Committee, issued the following statement praising President Trump's $1.5 trillion defense budget request for fiscal year 2027:

  • U.S. Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George to retire 'effective immediately,' Pentagon says Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- The Pentagon announced Thursday that Gen. Randy A. George will retire from his role as the 41st Chief of Staff of the Army "effective immediately," Anadolu Agency reported.
  • Smith Responds to Hegseth's Wartime Firing of Top Military Leaders House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 03 Apr 2026 -- House Armed Services Committee Ranking Member Adam Smith (D-Wash.) released the following statement in response to Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth's latest ouster of top military leadership.
  • Pentagon Fires Army Chief of Staff, 2 Generals in One Day Sputnik 03 Apr 2026 -- Chief Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell has confirmed the reports about the retirement of Gen. Randy George as the chief of staff of the army.
  • Come to Fight, Come to Win: II MEF First to Field New OSCAR Gen IV Training USMC News 03 Apr 2026 -- Leading the way for the Marine Corps, II Marine Expeditionary Force (II MEF) puts new doctrine into action by becoming the first command to implement the modernized Operational Stress Control and Readiness (OSCAR) training, certifying 30 new Core Master Trainers from March 24-26, 2026.
  • Motor Transport Unveils Integration Lab, Driving Tactical Wheeled Vehicle Modernization US Marine Corps News 03 Apr 2026 -- Previously, Marine Corps program offices integrating new systems onto the Joint Light Tactical Vehicle faced significant logistical challenges. The process involved requesting a vehicle from Program Executive Officer Land Systems' (PEO LS) Program Manager Motor Transport (PM Motor-T), costly and complex vehicle shipments, and potential roadblocks to project deadlines because of vehicle availability constraints. This system created inefficiencies and drove up costs.

  • What To Know About The F-15E Strike Eagle RFE/RL 03 Apr 2026 -- The F-15E Strike Eagle, which US officials say was shot down over Iran on April 3, is one of the US military's most advanced dual-role fighter aircraft, designed to carry out both air-to-air combat and deep-strike missions against ground targets.

  • Macron says the US' attitude is 'eroding NATO's very substance'; transatlantic alliance divisions deepen: Chinese expert Global Times 3 Apr 2026 -- French President Emmanuel Macron has sharply criticized the US for continuously casting doubt on NATO and making contradictory statements, saying, "We all need stability, calm, a return to peace - this isn't a show!"

  • Russian Aerospace Forces successfully launch Soyuz-2.1b missile carrier from Plesetsk Cosmodrome Russia MoD 03 Apr 2026 -- After the launch, the Soyuz-2.1a medium-class launch vehicle was escorted by ground-based automated control system of the German Titov Main Test Space Centre.

  • France to Fund Rafale F5 Project Independently Following Rift With UAE - Reports Sputnik 03 Apr 2026 -- France will be forced to independently finance its Rafale F5 fighter jet project due to disagreements with the United Arab Emirates over participation in the development, French newspaper La Tribune reported.

  • Indian Navy's latest stealth frigate 'INS Taragiri' commissioned in Visakhapatnam India PIB 03 Apr 2026 -- INS Taragiri, the fourth potent platform of the Project 17A class, was commissioned into the Indian Navy in the presence of Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh at Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh on April 03, 2026. A masterclass in modern naval shipbuilding, this latest stealth frigate, with a displacement of approximately 6,670 tonnes, has been designed by the Warship Design Bureau and built by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited, with the support of MSMEs, for multi-role operations. It utilises advanced stealth technology to achieve a significantly reduced radar signature, providing a lethal edge in contested environments.
  • Raksha Mantri lays foundation stone of Large Cavitation Tunnel at NSTL, Visakhapatnam to boost naval research & testing capabilities India PIB 03 Apr 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh, on April 03, 2026, laid the foundation stone of the state-of-the-art Large Cavitation Tunnel (LCT) facility at the Naval Science & Technological Laboratory (NSTL), a premium laboratory of DRDO in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. The facility will significantly enhance India's naval research and testing capabilities, marking a major step towards achieving technological self-reliance.

  • Azerbaijan, Türkiye launch implementation of MoU on defence industry cooperation Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- Azerbaijan's Deputy Minister of Defense Industry Hidayat Azimov met with a Turkish delegation led by Deputy Secretary of Corporate Development and Talent Management of the Secretariat of Defence Industries Hakan Karataş, who is on a working visit to the country.

  • Vietnam, Laos hold 6th Defence Policy Dialogue Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- The 6th Vietnam-Laos Defence Policy Dialogue took place in the central city of Da Nang on April 3, underscoring the commitment to deepening bilateral defence cooperation amid evolving regional and global security challenges, according to the Vietnam News Agency (VNA).

  • Servicemen of Combined Arms Army to participate in "Heydar Aliyev-2026" joint exercise Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- In alignment with the military cooperation agreement signed between Azerbaijan and Türkiye, a national flag change ceremony was conducted in preparation for the joint exercise "Heydar Aliyev-2026", which is scheduled to take place in Kars, Türkiye, Azerbaijan's Defense Ministry told AZERTAC.

  • Sweden to invest $916M in air defense systems Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- Sweden will invest 8.7 billion Swedish crowns ($916 million) in air defense systems, Anadolu Agency reported citing Swedish Defense Minister Pal Jonson.

  • Azerbaijan Minister of Defense attends meeting of CIS Council of Defense Ministers Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- The Minister of Defense of the Republic of Azerbaijan, Colonel General Zakir Hasanov, who is on an official visit to the Russian Federation, participated in the meeting of the Council of Defense Ministers of the Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS) member states, held in Moscow on April 2, Azerbaijan`s Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • EUNAVFOR ASPIDES: LUIGI RIZZO Close Protection European External Action Service (EEAS) 03 Apr 2026 -- Merchant vessels transiting the Red Sea completed their journey safely under the protection of Italian Frigate LUIGI RIZZO.

  • Deminers race to keep up with military technology UN News 03 Apr 2026 -- In conflict zones where new technologies are making landmines more dangerous, deminers must innovate at the same pace to avoid being left behind, a leading UN mines expert has told UN News.

  • READOUT: Ranking Member Shaheen and Senator Curtis's Bipartisan Delegation Travel to South Korea US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 03 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and John Curtis (R-UT) led a bipartisan Congressional delegation to South Korea for meetings with senior government officials, U.S. diplomatic and military leadership. The delegation included Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC) and Senator Jacky Rosen (D-NV).

  • Remarks by PM Netanyahu at the Kirya in Tel Aviv Israel - Prime Minister 03 Apr 2026 -- "Together with our American friends, we are continuing to crush the terrorist regime in Iran. We are eliminating commanders, and bombing bridges and infrastructure."

  • The OIC's Secretary-General Sends Letters to International Parties and Institutions Regarding the Knesset's Approval of the "Death Penalty for Palestinian Prisoners Law" Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 03 Apr 2026 -- The Secretary-General of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), Hissein Brahim Taha, sent identical letters to several international parties and institutions, including Mr
  • Legitimate rights of the Palestinian people should be respected and protected, Chinese FM responds to Israel passing the 'death penalty for terrorists' law on Palestinians Global Times 03 Apr 2026 -- The legitimate rights of the Palestinian people should be respected and protected. It is hoped that the relevant parties will stop actions that exacerbate tensions and intensify contradictions, China's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Friday, in response to foreign media's inquiry on Israel passing a legislation widely known as the "death penalty for terrorists" law on Palestinians.

    • Other Conflicts

  • China's mediation welcomed by Pakistan and Afghanistan as both agree to sit down again for negotiations: FM spokesperson Global Times 03 Apr 2026 -- Both Pakistan and Afghanistan value and welcome China's mediation efforts and are willing to sit down again for negotiations, which is a positive development，Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Friday, in response to media inquiries over the peace talks between Afghanistan and Pakistan held in China，and if China would offer any guarantees to prevent future conflicts.

  • Yemeni forces carry out third joint ballistic missile strike on vital Israeli targets Press TV 3 Apr 2026 -- The Yemeni Armed Forces have successfully executed a joint military operation targeting vital Israeli enemy positions in the occupied Yaffa area using a barrage of ballistic missiles.

  • 'We watched them die before our eyes': Sudan health workers helpless amid medical shortages UN News 03 Apr 2026 -- As violence forces tens of thousands to flee Sudan's South Kordofan state, doctors in a key maternity hospital are facing impossible choices - with too few supplies, too many patients, and lives slipping away.
  • 10 medical staff killed in drone strike on hospital in central Sudan Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- At least 10 medical and administrative staff were killed, and 22 civilians were injured on Thursday in a drone strike targeting Al-Jabalain Hospital in White Nile State, central Sudan, Xinhua reports citing the Sudanese Ministry of Health.

News Reports

  • U.S. Senate sends DHS funding bill back to House in attempt to end prolonged partial shutdown People's Daily 3 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Senate on Thursday sent its deal to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) back to the House in an attempt to end the prolonged partial shutdown.

  • Sanctioned ship suspected of causing oil spill in Baltic Sea Police of Finland 03 Apr 2026 -- Early on Friday, the Swedish Coast Guard ordered a ship suspected of causing an oil spill in the Baltic Sea, east of Gotland, to anchor in Swedish territorial waters. A prosecutor is leading a preliminary investigation into suspected environmental crimes. In connection with the detection, it was found that the ship is on the EU sanctions list and several unclear circumstances surrounds the vessel, including its flag status.

  • PH-Myanmar engagement continues after military leader's election win Philippine News Agency 03 Apr 2026 -- Malacañang on Friday said the Philippines will continue engaging with Myanmar following the election of military leader Min Aung Hlaing, who was elected president through a parliamentary vote.
  • Myanmar's former junta chief elected president by parliament Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 03 Apr 2026 -- Former Burmese military junta chief Min Aung Hlaing was elected Myanmar's 11th president on Friday after winning more than half the votes in parliament, Anadolu Agency reported.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with SCO Secretary General Nurlan Yermekbayev Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks during talks with SCO Secretary General Nurlan Yermekbayev, Moscow, April 3, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026
  • Russia Sees Attempts to Weaken SCO, Will Ensure They Do Not Succeed - Lavrov Sputnik 03 Apr 2026 -- Russia sees attempts to weaken the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) and will do everything to ensure they do not succeed, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Friday.
  • Telephone conversation with President of Turkiye Recep Tayyip Erdogan President of Russia 03 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with President of the Republic of Turkiye Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the initiative of the Turkish side.
  • Russia and Turkiye to Boost Joint Work on Black Sea Security Sputnik 03 Apr 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin and Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan discussed on Friday the implementation of joint strategic energy projects between the two countries, the Kremlin said.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Chair Wicker Press Administration for Answers on Sanctioned Russian Delegation Visit to United States US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 03 Apr 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Roger Wicker (R-MS), Chairman of the Senate Armed Services Committee, sent a letter to Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent raising serious concerns about the national security implications of a delegation of sanctioned members of the Russian Duma visiting the United States for meetings with Members of Congress and executive branch officials. The lawmakers warned that the delegation—comprised of Kremlin-aligned officials involved in cyberattacks, war crimes in Ukraine and support for Iran's targeting of U.S. personnel—posed significant counterintelligence risks and called into question the Administration's decision to waive sanctions to allow their entry. They also underscored that the visit came amid mounting evidence of Russia's support for Iran's military activities and continued aggression against Ukraine and NATO allies.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning's Regular Press Conference on April 3, 2026 PRC MofA 03 Apr 2026
  • HKSAR gov't files application with court to forfeit Jimmy Lai's offense-related properties People's Daily 3 Apr 2026 -- The Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) government on Thursday filed an application with the Court of First Instance for forfeiture of the offense-related properties of Jimmy Lai in order to achieve the important objectives of preventing and suppressing acts and activities endangering national security.

  • U.S. lawmakers introduce bipartisan bill to protect Taiwan's undersea cables CNA 03 Apr 2026 -- Three U.S. House representatives on Thursday introduced a bipartisan bill aimed at strengthening the resilience of Taiwan's undersea cables and other critical infrastructure against growing threats from China.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 03 Apr 2026

  • Enlarged Plenary Meetings of Pyongyang Municipal and Provincial Committees of WPK KCNA 03 Apr 2026 -- Enlarged plenary meetings of Pyongyang municipal and all provincial committees of the Workers' Party of Korea were held to carry through the important tasks set forth in the historic policy the respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made at the First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Visits Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at Overseas Military Operations under Construction KCNA 3 Apr 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un , General Secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and President of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, gave field guidance at the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations under construction.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Inspects Service Facilities in Fourth-Stage District of Hwasong Area Nearing Inauguration KCNA 3 Apr 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un , General Secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and President of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, went around various service facilities in the fourth-stage district of the Hwasong Area nearing their inauguration in order to learn about the preparations for their operation.

  • Iran - Arrest of Nasrin Sotoudeh (April 3, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026

  • 45th Meeting of the Council of the Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026 -- Pakistan, which currently holds the chairmanship of the Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization, today successfully chaired the 45th Council Meeting of the Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO-RATS) in Tashkent, Uzbekistan.

  • Cuban Government Announces Decision to Grant Pardons to More Than 2,000 Prisoners Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026 -- As part of the process of granting penitentiary benefits established by law, and in accordance with Article 90, subsection II of the Constitution of the Republic, the decision was made to release 2,010 convicted individuals through a pardon approved by the Cuban government.

  • Belarus Cancels Passport Of Nobel Laureate Byalyatski, Other Former Political Prisoners RFE/RL 03 Apr 2026 -- Belarus has cancelled the passport of Nobel Peace Prize laureate Ales Byalyatski and other recently deported political prisoners, in what rights groups say is a new form of pressure on exiled dissidents.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's talks with Minister of Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation and Egyptian Expatriates of the Arab Republic of Egypt Badr Abdelatty Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's statement and answers to media questions at a joint news conference following talks with Minister of Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation, and Egyptian Expatriates of the Arab Republic of Egypt Badr Abdelatty, Moscow, April 3, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks during talks with Minister of Foreign Affairs, Emigration and Egyptian Expatriates of the Arab Republic of Egypt, Badr Abdelatty, Moscow, April 3, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026

  • The Daily Press Briefing (04/03/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 03 Apr 2026

  • Rubio's claims on Panama ships 'completely groundless,' reveal intention to dominate canal: FM spokesperson Global Times 03 Apr 2026 -- Claims from the US side are completely groundless and a blatant reversal of right and wrong. The fact that the US keeps making irresponsible remarks only exposes its own intention to dominate the Panama Canal, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Friday, in response to media inquiry over remarks from US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who smeared China for so-called detaining or holding up Panama-flagged ships.
